title: Faqs_On_Recycling
text:
Please see our Household Hazardous Waste web page <<https://epa.gov/hw/household-hazardous-waste-hhw>>for more information on household hazardous wastes and tips for how to reduce it in your home.

## What should I do with paint? Barometers and thermometers? Burnt-out light bulbs?

*Paint:* Check local places that take donations (schools, places of faith, charities, non-profits) to see if they will accept paint donations, and contact your local recycling or household hazardous waste facility for ways you can recycle paint in your area.

*Barometers and thermometers:* Please avoid discarding them in the trash. Check with your local recycling or household hazardous waste facility, or visit Earth911 [Earth911](https://earth911.com/) for more information on ways to properly dispose them, as some thermometers are considered household hazardous waste.

*Burnt-out light bulbs:* Check with your local recycling facility for recycling options for burnt-out light bulbs, or take them to a retail store in your area that offers light bulb recycling.

Last updated on January 22, 2026
